parts.text:
This is a delicate balance to strike. You want to acknowledge the reality of the situation without sounding bitter, and you want to ask for help without sounding desperate.

Here are three options ranging from "short and direct" to "reflective and detailed."

### Option 1: Direct and Resilient (Best for a quick update)
**Headline: Some personal news: I’m currently looking for my next chapter.**

I’m sharing some tough news today: along with a number of my talented colleagues, I’ve been laid off from my role as **[Job Title]** at **[Company]**.

To be completely honest, it’s a difficult pill to swallow. I truly enjoyed the work we were doing and the team I was doing it with. But as much as this transition sucks, I’m choosing to focus on the gratitude I have for the experience and the skills I’m taking with me. 

I’m now looking for new opportunities in **[Industry/Field]**, specifically roles involving **[Skill A, Skill B, or Skill C]**. If you know of any openings or would be willing to connect me with someone in your network, I’d be incredibly grateful. 

Onward and upward. 

#OpenToWork #CareerTransition #[Industry]

***

### Option 2: Reflective and Personal (Best if you were there for a long time)
**Headline: Closing a chapter at [Company Name].**

After **[Number]** years at **[Company]**, my time there has unexpectedly come to an end due to a company-wide layoff. 

It’s a strange feeling to go from "full speed ahead" to a dead stop overnight. While the situation is definitely frustrating, I’m trying to keep perspective. I’m incredibly proud of **[mention one specific achievement, e.g., the product launch we completed last quarter]**, and even prouder of the relationships I built along the way. To my former colleagues: thank you for making the good days great and the hard days manageable.

I’m taking a few days to decompress and then I’ll be diving headfirst into the job search. I’m looking for **[Type of Role]** roles where I can help a team **[solve X problem or achieve Y goal]**.

If you have any leads or just want to grab a (virtual) coffee to catch up, please reach out!

#CareerUpdate #Networking #NewOpportunities

***

### Option 3: Short and Punchy (Best if you want to keep it professional and brief)
**Headline: Life update: I’m officially a "free agent."**

I’ve recently been impacted by the layoffs at **[Company]**. While it’s never easy to share news like this—and it certainly isn’t the news I hoped to be sharing this week—I’m looking forward to what’s next.

I’ve spent the last **[Timeframe]** honing my skills in **[Skillset]**, and I’m eager to bring that experience to a new team. 

If you’ve enjoyed working with me in the past or happen to hear of any roles that might be a fit for my background in **[Specific Field]**, I’d love to chat.

Thank you to everyone who has already reached out with kind words. It means a lot.

#OpenToWork #Hiring #JobSearch

***

### A few tips for the post:
*   **The "Open to Work" Photo Frame:** You can toggle this on in your profile settings. It’s very effective for the algorithm, even if you find it a bit "loud."
*   **The First Comment:** After you post, leave the first comment yourself with a link to your portfolio or a PDF of your resume. LinkedIn sometimes suppresses posts with external links in the main body.
*   **Tag People (Carefully):** If you have a few close colleagues who have already offered to help, tag them and thank them for their support. It helps the post's reach.
